description: PC vanilla TQ4_1S quantization + smoke test of Gemma 4 26B-A4B (MoE, 4B active) on RTX 5090

model.name: gemma-4-26B-A4B-it
model.source: hf:google/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it (fp16 safetensors) -> convert_hf_to_gguf.py --outtype f16 -> llama-quantize TQ4_1S

output_sample:
<|channel>thought
"The capital of France is"
The user is looking for the name of the capital city of France.
Paris.<channel|>Paris

vs_t0613_pc_dense_31b.note: MoE 4B-active routing yields 3.7x decode vs dense 31B at similar disk footprint

notes: C0 reference for PC MoE — P0 single-node, no compression beyond TQ4_1S quantization, no Flash-MoE slot-bank, no distributed.
Reused tqp-v0.1.0 side toolchain at ~/llama-cpp-v010/ from T-0607.pc.
30 decode tokens is a small sample; consider a longer-output prompt for tighter decode confidence intervals.
Source HF class is Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration (multimodal); converter handled text-only weight subset cleanly. Vision/audio not exercised.
All 658 tensors quantized cleanly including MoE expert tensors (ffn_gate_up_exps / ffn_down_exps).
VRAM headroom is tight (532 MiB free of 32,606); a longer context or batch may require KV reduction (TriAttention) or quantization headroom.
